  <outcome>"\"{\\\"result\\\":\\\"services/inputflinger/dispatcher/FocusResolver.h - platform/frameworks/native - Git at Googleandroid/platform/frameworks/native/refs/heads/main/./services/inputflinger/dispatcher/FocusResolver.hblob: 2910ba44c8e98e579f4d2cba0e9e65cd5ddc19c1 [file] [edit]/* * Copyright (C) 2021 The Android Open Source Project * * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\\\\\"License\\\\\\\"); * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License. * You may obtain a copy of the License at * * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 * * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\\\\\"AS IS\\\\\\\" BASIS, * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and * limitations under the License. */#pragma once#include <stdint.h>#include <optional>#include <unordered_map>#include <android/gui/FocusRequest.h>#include <binder/Binder.h>#include <gui/WindowInfo.h>namespace android::inputdispatcher {// Keeps track of the focused window per display. The class listens to updates from input dispatcher// and provides focus changes.//// Focus Policy// Window focusabilty - A window token can be focused if there is at least one window handle that// is visible with the same token and all window handles with the same token are focusable.// See FocusResolver::isTokenFocusable//// Focus request - Request will be granted if the window is focusable. If it's not// focusable, then the request is persisted and granted when it becomes focusable. The currently// focused window will lose focus and any pending keys will be added to a queue so it can be sent// to the window when it gets focus.//// Condition focus request - Request with a focus token specified. Request will be granted if the// window is focusable and the focus token is the currently focused. Otherwise, the request is// dropped. Conditional focus requests are not persisted. The window will lose focus and go back// to the focus token if it becomes not focusable.//// Window handle updates - Focus is lost when the currently focused window becomes not focusable.// If the previous focus request is focusable, then we will try to grant that window focus.class FocusResolver {public: // Returns the focused window token on the specified display. sp<IBinder> getFocusedWindowToken(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId) const; struct FocusChanges { sp<IBinder> oldFocus; sp<IBinder> newFocus; ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId; std::string reason; }; std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> setInputWindows( ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId, const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows); std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> setFocusedWindow( const android::gui::FocusRequest& request, const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows); // Display has been removed from the system, clean up old references. void displayRemoved(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId); // exposed for debugging bool hasFocusedWindowTokens() const { return !mFocusedWindowTokenByDisplay.empty(); } std::string dumpFocusedWindows() const; std::string dump() const;private: enum class Focusability { OK, NO_WINDOW, NOT_FOCUSABLE, NOT_VISIBLE, ftl_last = NOT_VISIBLE }; // Checks if the window token can be focused on a display. The token can be focused if there is // at least one window handle that is visible with the same token and all window handles with // the same token are focusable. // // In the case of mirroring, two windows may share the same window token and their visibility // might be different. Example, the mirrored window can cover the window its mirroring. However, // we expect the focusability of the windows to match since its hard to reason why one window // can receive focus events and the other cannot when both are backed by the same input channel. // static Focusability isTokenFocusable( const sp<IBinder>& token, const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows, sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>& outFocusableWindow); static FocusResolver::Focusability getResolvedFocusWindow( const sp<IBinder>& token, const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows, sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>& outFocusableWindow); // Focus tracking for keys, trackball, etc. A window token can be associated with one or // more InputWindowHandles. If a window is mirrored, the window and its mirror will share // the same token. Focus is tracked by the token per display and the events are dispatched // to the channel associated by this token. typedef std::pair<std::string /* name */, sp<IBinder>> NamedToken; std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, NamedToken> mFocusedWindowTokenByDisplay; // This map will store the focus request per display. When the input window handles are updated, // the current request will be checked to see if it can be processed at that time. std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, android::gui::FocusRequest> mFocusRequestByDisplay; // Last reason for not granting a focus request. This is used to add more debug information // in the event logs. std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, Focusability> mLastFocusResultByDisplay; std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> updateFocusedWindow( ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId, const std::string& reason, const sp<IBinder>& token, const std::string& tokenName = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"); std::optional<android::gui::FocusRequest> getFocusRequest(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId);};} // namespace android::inputdispatcher\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n[Content info: Showing characters 0-5582 of 5582 total]\\\"}\""</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

populateOverlayInputInfo(InputWindowHandleWrapper inputWindowHandle,\\n            WindowState w) {\\n        populateOverlayInputInfo(inputWindowHandle);\\n        inputWindowHandle.setTouchOcclusionMode(w.getTouchOcclusionMode());\\n    }\\n\\n    // This would reset InputWindowHandle fields to prevent it could be found by input event.\\n    // We need to check if any new field of InputWindowHandle could impact the result.\\n    @VisibleForTesting\\n    static void populateOverlayInputInfo(InputWindowHandleWrapper inputWindowHandle) {\\n        inputWindowHandle.setDispatchingTimeoutMillis(0); // It should never receive input.\\n        inputWindowHandle.setFocusable(false);\\n        // The input window handle without input channel must not have a token.\\n        inputWindowHandle.setToken(null);\\n        inputWindowHandle.setScaleFactor(1f);\\n        final int defaultType = WindowManager.LayoutParams.TYPE_APPLICATION;\\n The class listens to updates from input dispatcher\\n// and provides focus changes.\\n//\\n// Focus Policy\\n//   Window focusabilty - A window token can be focused if there is at least one window handle that\\n//   is visible with the same token and all window handles with the same token are focusable.\\n//   See FocusResolver::isTokenFocusable\\n//\\n//   Focus request - Request will be granted if the window is focusable. If it's not\\n//   focusable, then the request is persisted and granted when it becomes focusable. The currently\\n//   focused window will lose focus and any pending keys will be added to a queue so it can be sent\\n//   to the window when it gets focus.\\n//\\n//   Condition focus request - Request with a focus token specified. Request will be granted if the\\n//   window is focusable and the focus token is the currently focused. Otherwise, the request is\\n//   dropped. Conditional focus requests are not persisted. The window will lose focus and go back\\n//   to the focus token if it becomes not focusable.\\n//\\n//   Window handle updates - Focus is lost when the currently focused window becomes not focusable.\\n//   If the previous focus request is focusable, then we will try to grant that window focus.\\nclass FocusResolver {\\npublic:\\n    // Returns the focused window token on the specified display.\\n    sp<IBinder> getFocusedWindowToken(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId) const;\\n\\n    struct FocusChanges {\\n        sp<IBinder> oldFocus;\\n        sp<IBinder> newFocus;\\n        ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId;\\n        std::string reason;\\n    };\\n    std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> setInputWindows(\\n            ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId,\\n            const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows);\\n    std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> setFocusedWindow(\\n            const android::gui::FocusRequest& request,\\n            const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows);\\n\\n    // Display has been removed from the system, clean up old references.\\n    void displayRemoved(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId);\\n\\n    // exposed for debugging\\n    bool hasFocusedWindowTokens() const { return !mFocusedWindowTokenByDisplay.empty(); }\\n    std::string dumpFocusedWindows() const;\\n    std::string dump() const;\\n\\nprivate:\\n    enum class Focusability {\\n        OK,\\n        NO_WINDOW,\\n        NOT_FOCUSABLE,\\n        NOT_VISIBLE,\\n\\n        ftl_last = NOT_VISIBLE\\n    };\\n\\n    // Checks if the window token can be focused on a display. The token can be focused if there is\\n    // at least one window handle that is visible with the same token and all window handles with\\n    // the same token are focusable.\\n    //\\n    // In the case of mirroring, two windows may share the same window token and their visibility\\n    // might be different. Example, the mirrored window can cover the window its mirroring. However,\\n    // we expect the focusability of the windows to match since its hard to reason why one window\\n    // can receive focus events and the other cannot when both are backed by the same input channel.\\n    //\\n    static Focusability isTokenFocusable(\\n            const sp<IBinder>& token,\\n            const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows,\\n            sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>& outFocusableWindow);\\n\\n    static FocusResolver::Focusability getResolvedFocusWindow(\\n            const sp<IBinder>& token,\\n            const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows,\\n            sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>& outFocusableWindow);\\n\\n    // Focus tracking for keys, trackball, etc. A window token can be associated with one or\\n    // more InputWindowHandles. If a window is mirrored, the window and its mirror will share\\n    // the same token. Focus is tracked by the token per display and the events are dispatched\\n    // to the channel associated by this token.\\n    typedef std::pair<std::string /* name */, sp<IBinder>> NamedToken;\\n    std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, NamedToken>\\n            mFocusedWindowTokenByDisplay;\\n\\n    // This map will store the focus request per display. When the input window handles are updated,\\n    // the current request will be checked to see if it can be processed at that time.\\n    std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, android::gui::FocusRequest>\\n            mFocusRequestByDisplay;\\n\\n    // Last reason for not granting a focus request. This is used to add more debug information\\n    // in the event logs.\\n    std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, Focusability>\\n            mLastFocusResultByDisplay;\\n\\n    std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> updateFocusedWindow(\\n            ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId, const std::string& reason, const sp<IBinder>& token,\\n            const std::string& tokenName = \\\"\\\");\\n    std::optional<android::gui::FocusRequest> getFocusRequest(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId);\\n};\\n\\n} // namespace android::inputdispatcher\\n\",\"numLines\":129,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":129}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

The currently\\n//   focused window will lose focus and any pending keys will be added to a queue so it can be sent\\n//   to the window when it gets focus.\\n//\\n//   Condition focus request - Request with a focus token specified. Request will be granted if the\\n//   window is focusable and the focus token is the currently focused. Otherwise, the request is\\n//   dropped. Conditional focus requests are not persisted. The window will lose focus and go back\\n//   to the focus token if it becomes not focusable.\\n//\\n//   Window handle updates - Focus is lost when the currently focused window becomes not focusable.\\n//   If the previous focus request is focusable, then we will try to grant that window focus.\\nclass FocusResolver {\\npublic:\\n    // Returns the focused window token on the specified display.\\n    sp<IBinder> getFocusedWindowToken(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId) const;\\n\\n    struct FocusChanges {\\n        sp<IBinder> oldFocus;\\n        sp<IBinder> newFocus;\\n        ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId;\\n        std::string reason;\\n    };\\n    std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> setInputWindows(\\n            ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId,\\n            const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows);\\n    std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> setFocusedWindow(\\n            const android::gui::FocusRequest& request,\\n            const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows);\\n\\n    // Display has been removed from the system, clean up old references.\\n    void displayRemoved(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId);\\n\\n    // exposed for debugging\\n    bool hasFocusedWindowTokens() const { return !mFocusedWindowTokenByDisplay.empty(); }\\n    std::string dumpFocusedWindows() const;\\n    std::string dump() const;\\n\\nprivate:\\n    enum class Focusability {\\n        OK,\\n        NO_WINDOW,\\n        NOT_FOCUSABLE,\\n        NOT_VISIBLE,\\n\\n        ftl_last = NOT_VISIBLE\\n    };\\n\\n    // Checks if the window token can be focused on a display. The token can be focused if there is\\n    // at least one window handle that is visible with the same token and all window handles with\\n    // the same token are focusable.\\n    //\\n    // In the case of mirroring, two windows may share the same window token and their visibility\\n    // might be different. Example, the mirrored window can cover the window its mirroring. Focus is tracked by the token per display and the events are dispatched\\n    // to the channel associated by this token.\\n    typedef std::pair<std::string /* name */, sp<IBinder>> NamedToken;\\n    std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, NamedToken>\\n            mFocusedWindowTokenByDisplay;\\n\\n    // This map will store the focus request per display. When the input window handles are updated,\\n    // the current request will be checked to see if it can be processed at that time.\\n    std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, android::gui::FocusRequest>\\n            mFocusRequestByDisplay;\\n\\n    // Last reason for not granting a focus request.   <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"/home/cwtrocks/aosp/native/services/inputflinger/dispatcher/FocusResolver.h\",\"content\":\"/*\\n * Copyright (C) 2021 The Android Open Source Project\\n *\\n * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n * You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n *\\n *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n *\\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n * limitations under the License.\\n */\\n\\n#pragma once\\n\\n#include <stdint.h>\\n#include <optional>\\n#include <unordered_map>\\n\\n#include <android/gui/FocusRequest.h>\\n#include <binder/Binder.h>\\n#include <gui/WindowInfo.h>\\n\\nnamespace android::inputdispatcher {\\n\\n// Keeps track of the focused window per display. The class listens to updates from input dispatcher\\n// and provides focus changes.\\n//\\n// Focus Policy\\n//   Window focusabilty - A window token can be focused if there is at least one window handle that\\n//   is visible with the same token and all window handles with the same token are focusable.\\n//   See FocusResolver::isTokenFocusable\\n//\\n//   Focus request - Request will be granted if the window is focusable. If it's not\\n//   focusable, then the request is persisted and granted when it becomes focusable. The currently\\n//   focused window will lose focus and any pending keys will be added to a queue so it can be sent\\n//   to the window when it gets focus.\\n//\\n//   Condition focus request - Request with a focus token specified. Request will be granted if the\\n//   window is focusable and the focus token is the currently focused. Otherwise, the request is\\n//   dropped. Conditional focus requests are not persisted. The window will lose focus and go back\\n//   to the focus token if it becomes not focusable.\\n//\\n//   Window handle updates - Focus is lost when the currently focused window becomes not focusable.\\n//   If the previous focus request is focusable, then we will try to grant that window focus.\\nclass FocusResolver {\\npublic:\\n    // Returns the focused window token on the specified display.\\n    sp<IBinder> getFocusedWindowToken(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId) const;\\n\\n    struct FocusChanges {\\n        sp<IBinder> oldFocus;\\n        sp<IBinder> newFocus;\\n        ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId;\\n        std::string reason;\\n    };\\n    std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> setInputWindows(\\n            ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId,\\n            const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows);\\n    std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> setFocusedWindow(\\n            const android::gui::FocusRequest& request,\\n            const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows);\\n\\n    // Display has been removed from the system, clean up old references.\\n    void displayRemoved(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId);\\n\\n    // exposed for debugging\\n    bool hasFocusedWindowTokens() const { return !mFocusedWindowTokenByDisplay.empty(); }\\n    std::string dumpFocusedWindows() const;\\n    std::string dump() const;\\n\\nprivate:\\n    enum class Focusability {\\n        OK,\\n        NO_WINDOW,\\n        NOT_FOCUSABLE,\\n        NOT_VISIBLE,\\n\\n        ftl_last = NOT_VISIBLE\\n    };\\n\\n    // Checks if the window token can be focused on a display. The token can be focused if there is\\n    // at least one window handle that is visible with the same token and all window handles with\\n    // the same token are focusable.\\n    //\\n    // In the case of mirroring, two windows may share the same window token and their visibility\\n    // might be different. Example, the mirrored window can cover the window its mirroring. However,\\n    // we expect the focusability of the windows to match since its hard to reason why one window\\n    // can receive focus events and the other cannot when both are backed by the same input channel.\\n    //\\n    static Focusability isTokenFocusable(\\n            const sp<IBinder>& token,\\n            const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows,\\n            sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>& outFocusableWindow);\\n\\n    static FocusResolver::Focusability getResolvedFocusWindow(\\n            const sp<IBinder>& token,\\n            const std::vector<sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>>& windows,\\n            sp<android::gui::WindowInfoHandle>& outFocusableWindow);\\n\\n    // Focus tracking for keys, trackball, etc. A window token can be associated with one or\\n    // more InputWindowHandles. If a window is mirrored, the window and its mirror will share\\n    // the same token. Focus is tracked by the token per display and the events are dispatched\\n    // to the channel associated by this token.\\n    typedef std::pair<std::string /* name */, sp<IBinder>> NamedToken;\\n    std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, NamedToken>\\n            mFocusedWindowTokenByDisplay;\\n\\n    // This map will store the focus request per display. When the input window handles are updated,\\n    // the current request will be checked to see if it can be processed at that time.\\n    std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, android::gui::FocusRequest>\\n            mFocusRequestByDisplay;\\n\\n    // Last reason for not granting a focus request. This is used to add more debug information\\n    // in the event logs.\\n    std::unordered_map<ui::LogicalDisplayId /* displayId */, Focusability>\\n            mLastFocusResultByDisplay;\\n\\n    std::optional<FocusResolver::FocusChanges> updateFocusedWindow(\\n            ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId, const std::string& reason, const sp<IBinder>& token,\\n            const std::string& tokenName = \\\"\\\");\\n    std::optional<android::gui::FocusRequest> getFocusRequest(ui::LogicalDisplayId displayId);\\n};\\n\\n} // namespace android::inputdispatcher\\n\",\"numLines\":129,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":129}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

\\\\\\\"https://blog.csdn.net/u010873775/article/details/70477185?locationNum=10&fps=1\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"snippet\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"版权声明:本文为博主原创文章,遵循 CC 4.0 BY-SA 版权协议,转载请附上原文出处链接和本声明。 本文链接:https://blog.csdn.net/u010873775/article/details/70477185 android studio升级到2.3,然后打包发布程序时,在android 7.0 安装的好好的,其他版本居然出错(我在华为进行的兼容性测试),回想了一下我升级的内容,经过一番google,把问题定位在android studio最后的签名版本选择上。 这是官方说明: Android 7.0 引入一项新的应用签名方案 APK Signature Scheme v2,它能提供更快的应用安装时间和更多针对未授权 APK 文件更改的保护。在默认情况下,Android Studio 2.2 和Android Plugin for Gradle 2.2 会使用 APK Signature Scheme v2 和传统签名方案来签署您的应用。 想要兼容低版本,直接在Signature Versions 勾选V1和V2即可。 参考链接: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/2914105/what-is-install-parse-failed-no-certificates-error http://blog.csdn.net/yingpaixiaochuan/article/details/60964152 http://blog.csdn.net/lvshuchangyin/article/details/62227286\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"date\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"2017-04-22 21:18:13\\\\\\\"\\\\n    },\\\\n    {\\\\n      \\\\\\\"title\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"Android焦点窗口_android 焦点-CSDN博客\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"link\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"https://blog.csdn.net/qq_41095045/article/details/139686634\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"snippet\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"概述 何为焦点窗口?顾名思义就是有焦点窗口,要注意和view的焦点概念要做区分,两个不是一个层面上的概念。为什么窗口要拥有焦点呢,下面都是我的理解,有不对的地方欢迎讨论:焦点窗口主要使用在InputDispachar分发按键事件——keyevent阶段,在keyevent分发阶段,此时的焦点窗口就是该事件应该分发给的窗口。不像触摸事件,在分发过程中可以通过触摸的坐标推断给事件应该分发给那个窗口,keyeven来自物理按键,没有对应的坐标,只能通过焦点窗口确定发送给那个窗口。 解释完毕焦点窗口的作用,在来简单介绍下一种常见的ANR,即no focus anr,这种anr发生就是因为在分发一次keyEvent时,没有找到焦点窗口,便开始了anr计时,5s之后,分发事件的过程中发依旧没有对应的焦点窗口,便会发生无焦点ANR。 焦点窗口的更新流程在Android12之后,涉及到了三个模块:WMS, Surfaceflinger, InputDispachar,下文将会分析具体得人更新焦点窗口流程。 首先明确三个概念:1. 顶部焦点屏幕:mTopFocusedDisplayId,这个变量的声明位于窗口的根节点RootWindowContainer中. 这个变量代表了响应没有指明屏幕的key或者pointer类型的event的屏幕的id。 frameworks/base/services/core/java/com/android/server/wm/RootWindowContainer.java // The ID of the display which is responsible for receiving display-unspecified key and pointer // events. private int mTopFocusedDisplayId = INVALID_DISPLAY; 运行项目并下载源码java运行123452. 焦点app:mFocusedApp代表了一个DisplayContent上正处于前台的ActivityRecord,一般这个ActivityRecord下的窗口将会成为焦点窗口,存在当焦点app属于该ActivityRecord,但是焦点窗口不属于该ActivityRecord下属的窗口的情况。 frameworks/bas\\\\\\\",\\\\n \\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\"\\\\n  }\\\\n}\\\",\\\"annotations\\\":null,\\\"_meta\\\":null}\""</outcome>

\\\\\\\"snippet\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"这里在安装Android studio3.3的时候,遇到安装“SDK出现Android Support Repository, SDK Patch Applier v3 and 3 more SDK components were not installed…”情况 解决办法是 1、点击Cancel先取消 如果您不喜欢使用gedit命令,而且当前帐户为非root帐户,那么可把/etc/hosts复制到桌面上,然后手动编辑后保存,再使用命令copy覆盖过去即可,示例如下: sudo cp hosts /etc/* 3、 用记事本打开,添加 4.重启Android studio,即可开始继续下载 补充: 若遇到不能进行勾选的时候,可以先关闭,然后以管理员的方式打开即可\\\\\\\",\\\\n window中查看mCurrentFocus和mFocusedApp,也可以通过如下shell命令来查看当前的FocusWindow: mCurrentFocus=Window{f96644 u0 NotificationShade} mFocusedApp=ActivityRecord{e9566ee u0 com.android.launcher3/.uioverrides.QuickstepLauncher} t12} mCurrentFocus指的是当前的焦点窗口 mFocusedApp指的是当前的焦点Activity $ adb shell dumpsys window d | grep \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"mCurrentFocus\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" mCurrentFocus=Window{135c912 mode=0 rootTaskId=4 u0 com.example.myapplication/com.example.myapplication.MainActivity}一键获取完整项目代码 InputWindow是指能接收input事件的窗口,当WMS中状态发生变化后,会将所有符合条件的窗口设置给底层InputFlinger中,在派发事件时,将对从这些窗口中选择目标窗口进行派发,这些窗口就是InputWindow｡\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"date\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"2024-04-09 13:25:53\\\\\\\"\\\\n WMS通知到SurfaceFlinger4.2.1.2 SurfaceFlinger 到InputDIspatcher4.2.2 请求窗口焦点4.2.2.1 WMS通知到SurfaceFlinger4.2.2.2 SurfaceFlinger 到InputDIspatcher5 打印 Focus entering\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" 或Focus leaving6 堆栈6.1 setFocusApp 讲解 焦点窗口如何显示以及如何查看当前焦点窗口 相关知识点 SurfaceFlinger是Android系统中负责合成各个层(Layer)并将其显示到屏幕上的服务。应用通过SurfaceComposerClient与SurfaceFlinger通信,Transaction则是用来收集一批图层状态变更,然后一次性提交,这样可以提高效率,减少多次IPC调用的开销涉及到的类如下 studio开发中,有可能会出现 Installation failed since app's minSdkVersion (API 21) is newer than device's API level (API 19). Open the project structure dialog to edit the minSdkVersion? 这样的问题,形如 翻译过来就是, 安装失败,因为app的minSdkVersion (API 21)比设备的API级别(API 19)更新。 打开项目结构对话框编辑minSdkVersion? 再翻译一遍就是,你的sdk的版本比较低,不能安装这个高版本的软件, 再再翻译一遍就是,app的版本是Android 5.0 你不能用Android 4.4.2来运行,什么你问我怎么知道版本的 好了,你知道了,现在解决问题的方案就是跟你的计算机安装上比较高一点的Android版本,也就是安装高版本的api, 那么怎么安装呢? 很简单啦, 首先打开Android studio ,左上角点file,再点setting,然后出现下面的这个界面, 根据上面的1.2.3进入右边你想下载的版本sdk,给自己想要的版本打上勾,点ok,之后就是一连串的司空见惯的操作, 就可以安装完毕了,下一次运行的时候,记得选择你希望的版本运行你的程序, 祝你成功!!!\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"date\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"2019-06-08 23:56:00\\\\\\\"\\\\n    },\\\\n    {\\\\n      \\\\\\\"title\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"安装APK在android7.0以下版本出现INSTALL_PARSE_FAILED_NO_CERTIFICATES错误的解决方案 - 擦肩的阳光的专栏 - CSDN博客\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"link\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"https://blog.csdn.net/u010873775/article/details/70477185?locationNum=10&fps=1\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"snippet\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"版权声明:本文为博主原创文章,遵循 CC 4.0 BY-SA 版权协议,转载请附上原文出处链接和本声明。 本文链接:https://blog.csdn.net/u010873775/article/details/70477185 android studio升级到2.3,然后打包发布程序时,在android 7.0 安装的好好的,其他版本居然出错(我在华为进行的兼容性测试),回想了一下我升级的内容,经过一番google,把问题定位在android studio最后的签名版本选择上。 这是官方说明: Android 7.0 引入一项新的应用签名方案 APK Signature Scheme v2,它能提供更快的应用安装时间和更多针对未授权 APK 文件更改的保护。在默认情况下,Android Studio 2.2 和Android Plugin for Gradle 2.2 会使用 APK Signature Scheme v2 和传统签名方案来签署您的应用。 想要兼容低版本,直接在Signature Versions 勾选V1和V2即可。 参考链接: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/2914105/what-is-install-parse-failed-no-certificates-error http://blog.csdn.net/yingpaixiaochuan/article/details/60964152 http://blog.csdn.net/lvshuchangyin/article/details/62227286\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"date\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"2017-04-22 21:18:13\\\\\\\"\\\\n    },\\\\n    {\\\\n      \\\\\\\"title\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"Android焦点窗口_android 焦点-CSDN博客\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"link\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"https://blog.csdn.net/qq_41095045/article/details/139686634\\\\\\\",\\\\n      \\\\\\\"snippet\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"概述 何为焦点窗口?顾名思义就是有焦点窗口,要注意和view的焦点概念要做区分,两个不是一个层面上的概念。为什么窗口要拥有焦点呢,下面都是我的理解,有不对的地方欢迎讨论:焦点窗口主要使用在InputDispachar分发按键事件——keyevent阶段,在keyevent分发阶段,此时的焦点窗口就是该事件应该分发给的窗口。不像触摸事件,在分发过程中可以通过触摸的坐标推断给事件应该分发给那个窗口,keyeven来自物理按键,没有对应的坐标,只能通过焦点窗口确定发送给那个窗口。 解释完毕焦点窗口的作用,在来简单介绍下一种常见的ANR,即no focus anr,这种anr发生就是因为在分发一次keyEvent时,没有找到焦点窗口,便开始了anr计时,5s之后,分发事件的过程中发依旧没有对应的焦点窗口,便会发生无焦点ANR。 焦点窗口的更新流程在Android12之后,涉及到了三个模块:WMS, Surfaceflinger, InputDispachar,下文将会分析具体得人更新焦点窗口流程。 首先明确三个概念:1. 顶部焦点屏幕:mTopFocusedDisplayId,这个变量的声明位于窗口的根节点RootWindowContainer中. 这个变量代表了响应没有指明屏幕的key或者pointer类型的event的屏幕的id。 frameworks/base/services/core/java/com/android/server/wm/RootWindowContainer.java // The ID of the display which is responsible for receiving display-unspecified key and pointer // events. private int mTopFocusedDisplayId = INVALID_DISPLAY; 运行项目并下载源码java运行123452. 焦点app:mFocusedApp代表了一个DisplayContent上正处于前台的ActivityRecord,一般这个ActivityRecord下的窗口将会成为焦点窗口,存在当焦点app属于该ActivityRecord,但是焦点窗口不属于该ActivityRecord下属的窗口的情况。 frameworks/bas\\\\\\\",\\\\n And then its waiting for you to put a UI up.\\\\n\\\\n7. android-sdk-sources-for-api-level-28/android/view/FocusFinder.java at ...\\\\n   URL: https://github.com/AndroidSDKSources/android-sdk-sources-for-api-level-28/blob/master/android/view/FocusFinder.java\\\\n   Summary: History 983 lines (886 loc) · 37.9 KB masterandroid-sdk-sources-for-api-level-28 /android/ view / FocusFinder.javaTop Code Blame 983 lines (886 loc) · 37.9 KB ...\\\\n\\\\n8. android - How to find out which view is focused? - Stack Overflow\\\\n   URL: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/5352209/how-to-find-out-which-view-is-focused\\\\n   Summary: I need to find out if any view is focused inside an Activity and what view it is. How to do this?\\\\n\\\\n9. Display support - Android Open Source Project\\\\n   URL: https://source.android.com/docs/core/display/multi_display/displays\\\\n   Summary: Updates made to these display-specific areas are provided below: Resizing activities and displays Display sizes and aspect ratios Display policies Display window settings Static display identifiers Using more than two displays Per-display focus Resize activities and displays To indicate that an app may not support multi-window mode or resizing, activities use the resizeableActivity=false ...\\\\n\\\\n10. Open the app from the desktop, when the window appears, immediately use ...\\\\n   URL: https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/260203909\\\\n   Summary: The real focus window is stored inFocusResolver.mFocusedWindowTokenByDisplay. When the user uses the return gesture to return during the process of opening the app, if the app window becomes the real focus window too slowly, the return gesture will fail, especially For windows with colorMode=COLOR_MODE_WIDE_COLOR_GAMUT (such as chrome) log:\\\\n\\\"}\""</outcome>

  <parameters>"{\"url\":\"https://android.googlesource.com/platform/frameworks/native/+/main/services/surfaceflinger/SurfaceFlinger.cpp?format=TEXT#1000\",\"prompt\":\"Search for \\\"updateInputFlinger\\\" method definition. Find all methods that call setFocusedWindow or IInputFlinger. Extract the relevant code sections.\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"bytes\":553712,\"code\":200,\"codeText\":\"OK\",\"result\":\"Based on the search results and the provided C++ source content from Android's SurfaceFlinger, here's what I found regarding input-related methods and connections to `IInputFlinger`:\\n\\n**Relevant findings from the source code:**\\n\\n1. **Include headers present in the file:**\\n   - `#include <android/os/IInputFlinger.h>`\\n   - `#include <gui/IWindow.h>`\\n   - `#include <gui/ISurfaceComposer.h>`\\n\\n2. **Key method: `bootFinished()`** — This is where the InputFlinger connection is established. In the `bootFinished()` method, the code waits for the window manager service and then:\\n   - Calls `defaultServiceManager()->getService(String16(\\\"inputflinger\\\"))` to retrieve the InputFlinger service\\n   - Obtains the input device's PID and UID\\n   - Checks permissions via `PermissionCache::checkPermission()`\\n   - Sets up the binder status with `enterBinderSterilization`\\n\\n3. **`setFocusedWindow` / `setInputWindows`**: The source references `setFocusedWindow` indirectly through the `InputDisplayState` and `IWindow` connection flow. The `WindowInfoHandle` and `ISurfaceComposer::LayerSettings` structures are used to propagate focus state through the pipeline.\\n\\n4. **`mInputFlinger` usage**: Throughout the code, input-related operations are routed through `mInputFlinger` — for example, when handling `onScreenModeChanged`, `onDisplayDeadlineExtensionChanged`, and other display/input events.\\n\\nThe InputFlinger binder interface is used to validate calling identity and permissions rather than as a direct method-call target in the visible portions of this file.\",\"durationMs\":26196,\"url\":\"https://android.googlesource.com/platform/frameworks/native/+/main/services/surfaceflinger/SurfaceFlinger.cpp?format=TEXT#1000\"}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

The content is truncated, so the definition may exist outside the visible range. The snippet cuts off mid-method during the `getHdrCapabilities` return statement.\\n\\n### `setFocusedWindow` References\\n\\n`setFocusedWindow` is not directly called in the visible portion of `SurfaceFlinger.cpp`. It is typically a method on `IInputFlinger` or related input interfaces (e.g., `IInputFlinger::setFocusedWindow`).\\n\\n### Key Observation\\n\\nThe critical binding pattern is:\\n\\n```cpp\\nsp<IInputFlinger> input = ...\\nmInputFlinger = interface_cast<os::IInputFlinger>(input);\\n```\\n\\nThis cast connects SurfaceFlinger to the system input service through the HAL interface defined in `android-os/IInputFlinger.h`.\",\"durationMs\":26948,\"url\":\"https://android.googlesource.com/platform/frameworks/native/+/main/services/surfaceflinger/SurfaceFlinger.cpp?format=TEXT#2000\"}"</outcome>

  <what_happened>mcp__fetch__imageFetch</what_happened>

  <parameters>"{\"url\":\"https://jishuzhan.net/article/1812320975358791681\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Contents of https://jishuzhan.net/article/1812320975358791681: Android焦点之SurfaceFlinger传递给InputFinger - 技术栈:\\n![](https://i-blog.csdnimg.cn/direct/7e8ee54259154af485fb385ae9b4a17e.png)\\n\\n接下来就到了SurfaceFlinger端，内调ISurfaceComposer的setTransactionState()\\n\\nSurfaceFlinger::setTransactionState:这里是一个BpBn操作，进程surfaceflinger的binder线程，主要是调用到SurfaceFlinger.cpp的setTransactionState()。\\n\\n在每次SurfaceFlinger主线程进行commit操作的时候，都会调用一次updateInputFlinger方法去更新一遍需要派发给InputDispatcher的窗口信息，代码如下：\\n\\nSurfaceFlinger::updateInputFlinger:\\n\\n需要出现在InputDispatcher的窗口列表中，在SurfaceFlinger这里需要满足两个条件：\\n\\n对应Layer存在于SurfaceFlinger所维护的Layer列表中，\\n\\n对应Layer需要满足needsInputInfo条件。\\n\\n对于第一个条件，当Layer对应Surface没有被上层destroy时，才会存在于SurfaceFlinger的Layer列表中。（上层移除Surface的时机一般为对应窗口退出动画执行完毕时，由relayoutWindow流程发起）\\n\\n对于第二个条件，需要对应窗口的inputChannelToken建立且没有被移除。\\n\\nLayer::fillInputInfo 设置可见性, 对于具有InputInfo的Layer，走的是canReceiveInput的逻辑判断当前是否可见：\\n\\n这里通过三个条件作可见性判断:\\n\\nisHiddenByPolicy:这里会检查目标Layer的flag是否带有不可见标识\\n\\nmBufferInfo.mBuffer:buffer为空即满足可见条件\\n\\ngetAlpha:Alpha值大于0，非透明即可见\\n\\n详细的有关layer可见性的本篇不再继续跟踪\\n\\nWindowInfosListenerInvoker::windowInfosChanged: windowInfosChanged方法能够将WindowInfo的变化通知到InputDispatcher。\\n\\n内调InputDispatcher.cpp内部类DispatcherWindowListener的onWindowInfosChanged();\\n\\n遍历,handlesPerDisplay,调setInputWindowsLocked() 由sf侧传递给input侧\\n\\njava 复制代码\\n\\n```\\n@frameworks/native/services/surfaceflinger/SurfaceFlinger.cpp\\nSurfaceFlinger::setTransactionState\\n    queueTransaction(state);  // 将TransactionState入队，该方法会触发相应的Commit操作\\n        mTransactionQueue.emplace(state);\\n        \\nSurfaceFlinger::onMessageInvalidate   //最新版本改成SurfaceFlinger::commit\\n    updateInputFlinger();\\n        if (mVisibleRegionsDirty || mInputInfoChanged) {\\n            mInputInfoChanged = false;\\n            notifyWindowInfos(); \\n                mDrawingState.traverseInReverseZOrder([&](Layer* layer) {\\n                    sp<DisplayDevice> display = enablePerWindowInputRotation() ? ON_MAIN_THREAD(getDisplayWithInputByLayer(layer)) : nullptr;     \\n@frameworks/native/services/surfaceflinger/Layer.cpp                    \\n                    windowInfos.push_back(layer->fillInputInfo(display));\\n                        WindowInfo info = mDrawingState.inputInfo;\\n                        info.visible = hasInputInfo() ? canReceiveInput() : isVisible();  // 设置WindowInfo可见性, 对于具不具备InputInfo的layer，有两种可见性的判断逻辑，对于输入窗口，走canReceiveInput\\n                            return !isHiddenByPolicy();  //isHiddenByPolicy:这里会检查目标Layer的flag是否带有不可见标识\\n                                return s.flags & layer_state_t::eLayerHidden;\\n@frameworks/native/services/surfaceflinger/WindowInfosListenerInvoker.cpp\\n                //通知InputDispatcher更新窗口列表信息                  \\n                mWindowInfosListenerInvoker->windowInfosChanged(windowInfos, mInputWindowCommands.syncInputWindows);  \\n                // listener其实就是InputDispather\\n                for (const auto& listener : windowInfosListeners) { listener->onWindowInfosChanged(windowInfos, shouldSync ? mWindowInfosReportedListener : nullptr);   }\\n@frameworks/native/services/inputflinger/dispatcher/InputDispatcher.cpp\\n                    //确保我们为所有现有显示器创建了一个条目，以便如果 displayId 没有窗口，我们可以知道窗口已从显示器中删除。\\n                    for (const auto& info : windowInfos) {\\n                        handlesPerDisplay.emplace(info.displayId, std::vector<sp<WindowInfoHandle>>());\\n                        handlesPerDisplay[info.displayId].push_back(new WindowInfoHandle(info));}\\n                    setInputWindows(handlesPerDisplay);                        \\n                        for (const auto& [displayId, handles] : handlesPerDisplay) { setInputWindowsLocked(handles, displayId); } //遍历,handlesPerDisplay,调setInputWindowsLocked()\\n        }\\n        // focusRequests也是从InputMonitor传过来的，InputMonitor的updateInputFocusRequest方法最后会调用requestFocus方法，requestFocus方法里面会往InputTransaction里设置focus window。\\n        for (const auto& focusRequest : inputWindowCommands.focusRequests) { mInputFlinger->setFocusedWindow(focusRequest); }\\n```\\n\\n<e>Content truncated. 83055 characters of text remaining. Call the imageFetch tool with start_index=20000 and/or imageStartIndex=0 to get more content.</e>\"}]"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

  <working_directory>/home/cwtrocks/aosp-explore</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"command\":\"python3 << 'EOF'\\nimport json\\nimport re\\n\\nwith open('/home/cwtrocks/.claude/projects/-home-cwtrocks-aosp-explore/70e5babe-c249-4678-b2c2-840aa31ee8c4/tool-results/call_9e0dcc19520441a7ab0f7a59.json', 'r') as f:\\n    data = json.load(f)\\n\\nitem = data[0]\\ntext = item['text']\\n\\n# The raw content is base64 encoded C++ source\\n# Find the base64 part after \\\"raw content:\\\\n\\\"\\nparts = text.split('raw content:\\\\n', 1)\\nif len(parts) == 2:\\n    # The base64 content may span multiple lines or be wrapped\\n    b64_lines = []\\n    remaining = parts[1].strip()\\n    # Try to extract base64 lines (may have newlines in between)\\n    lines = remaining.split('\\\\n')\\n    for line in lines[:3]:  # first few lines\\n        b64_lines.append(line.strip())\\n    # Join first few lines to try decoding\\n    test = '\\\\n'.join(b64_lines)\\n    print(f\\\"Testing decode of: {test[:200]}...\\\")\\n    try:\\n        import base64\\n        decoded = base64.b64decode(test).decode('utf-8', errors='replace')\\n        print(f\\\"Decoded first few lines: {decoded[:500]}\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"Decode error: {e}\\\")\\n        # Try another approach - maybe the whole thing is b64\\n        try:\\n            # The entire remaining text might be the b64\\n            decoded = base64.b64decode(remaining.replace('\\\\n', '')).decode('utf-8', errors='replace')\\n            print(f\\\"Full decode worked, length: {len(decoded)}\\\")\\n        except Exception as e2:\\n            print(f\\\"Full decode error: {e2}\\\")\\nEOF\\n\",\"description\":\"Decode base64 content properly\"}"</parameters>

(WindowInfo& windowInfo : windowInfos) {\\n        if (!windowInfo.inputConfig.test(WindowInfo::InputConfig::NOT_VISIBLE)) {\\n            visibleWindowIds.insert(windowInfo.id);\\n        }\\n    }\\n    bool visibleWindowsChanged = false;\\n    if (visibleWindowIds != mVisibleWindowIds) {\\n        visibleWindowsChanged = true;\\n        mVisibleWindowIds = std::move(visibleWindowIds);\\n visibleWindowsChanged, vsyncId,\\n                                                      frameTime]() mutable {\\n        SFTRACE_NAME(\\\"BackgroundExecutor::updateInputFlinger\\\");\\n        if    frameTime.ns()},\\n                                         std::move(\\n                                                 inputWindowCommands.windowInfosReportedListeners),\\n If there are listeners but no changes to input windows, call the listeners\\n            // immediately.\\n            for (const auto& listener : inputWindowCommands.windowInfosReportedListeners) {\\n                if (IInterface::asBinder(listener)->isBinderAlive()) {\\n                    listener->onWindowInfosReported();\\n                }\\n            }\\n        }\\n        for (const auto& focusRequest : inputWindowCommands.focusRequests) {\\n            inputFlinger->setFocusedWindow(focusRequest);\\n        }\\n    }});\\n\\n    mInputWindowCommands.clear();\\n}\\n\\nvoid SurfaceFlinger::persistDisplayBrightness(bool needsComposite) {\\n    const bool supportsDisplayBrightnessCommand = getHwComposer().getComposer()->isSupported(\\n            Hwc2::Composer::OptionalFeature::DisplayBrightnessCommand);\\n    if (!supportsDisplayBrightnessCommand) {\\n        return;\\n    }\\n\\n    for (const auto& [_, display] : FTL_FAKE_GUARD(mStateLock, mDisplays)) {\\n        if (const auto brightness = display->getStagedBrightness(); brightness) {\\n            if (!needsComposite) {\\n                const status_t error =\\n                        getHwComposer()\\n                                .setDisplayBrightness(display->getPhysicalId(), *brightness,\\n                                                      display->getCompositionDisplay()\\n                                                              ->getState()\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>
